member.name: T:Gurung.EfBulkOperations.PostgreSql.Handlers.PostgreSqlHandlers
member.summary: Provides static methods for performing high-performance bulk operations with PostgreSQL, including bulk insert, update, and merge operations using the COPY command and temporary tables.
member.remarks: This class is designed to facilitate efficient data manipulation in PostgreSQL databases by leveraging native bulk operations. It supports scenarios such as bulk inserting entities, copying data to temporary tables, and executing upsert (merge) operations. All methods are thread-safe as they do not maintain internal state. Exceptions are thrown as InvalidOperationException with additional context for easier troubleshooting. The class requires Npgsql and assumes that the provided connection and transaction are valid and open.

member.name: M:Gurung.EfBulkOperations.PostgreSql.Handlers.PostgreSqlHandlers.GetFilteredProperties(Gurung.EfBulkOperations.TableDetails,Gurung.EfBulkOperations.BulkConfig)
member.summary: Gets filtered properties based on identity columns and KeepIdentity configuration. Filters out identity columns when KeepIdentity is false.
member.param: Table information containing property and entity metadata.
Bulk configuration with KeepIdentity setting.

member.name: M:Gurung.EfBulkOperations.PostgreSql.Handlers.PostgreSqlHandlers.BulkInsertAsync``1(Npgsql.NpgsqlConnection,System.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable{``0},Gurung.EfBulkOperations.TableDetails,Gurung.EfBulkOperations.BulkConfig,System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Performs a bulk insert operation into a PostgreSQL table using binary import for the specified entities.
member.remarks: This method uses PostgreSQL's binary COPY protocol for efficient bulk data loading. Identity columns are handled according to the provided BulkConfig. The method does not automatically close the provided connection. Ensure that the connection remains open for the duration of the operation.
member.typeparam: The type of the entities to insert. Each entity must have properties that map to the columns defined in the target table.
member.param: The open NpgsqlConnection to the PostgreSQL database where the data will be inserted.
The collection of entities to insert into the database. Each entity represents a row to be added.
The table details, including schema, table name, and column mappings, that define the target table for the bulk insert.
The configuration options for the bulk insert operation. If null, default settings are used.
A token to monitor for cancellation requests. The operation is canceled if the token is triggered.
member.returns: A task that represents the asynchronous operation. The task result contains the NpgsqlBinaryImporter used for the bulk insert. The caller is responsible for disposing the importer after use.
member.exception: Thrown if the bulk copy operation fails due to an error during the insert process.

member.name: M:Gurung.EfBulkOperations.PostgreSql.Handlers.PostgreSqlHandlers.BulkCopyToTempTableAsync``1(Npgsql.NpgsqlConnection,Npgsql.NpgsqlTransaction,System.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable{``0},Gurung.EfBulkOperations.TableDetails,Gurung.EfBulkOperations.BulkConfig,System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Asynchronously copies a collection of entities to a temporary table in PostgreSQL using binary import for efficient bulk operations.
member.remarks: This method uses PostgreSQL's binary import to efficiently insert large numbers of entities into a temporary table. The operation is optimized for performance and is suitable for scenarios where high-throughput data loading is required. The method respects identity column handling based on the provided BulkConfig. The caller is responsible for ensuring that the connection is open and that the temporary table schema matches the entity structure.
member.typeparam: The type of the entities to be copied to the temporary table.
member.param: The open NpgsqlConnection to the PostgreSQL database where the temporary table resides.
The NpgsqlTransaction to associate with the bulk copy operation. Can be null if no transaction is required.
The collection of entities to copy into the temporary table. Each entity represents a row to be inserted.
The TableDetails object containing metadata about the target temporary table, including column mappings and entity type information.
The BulkConfig object specifying options for the bulk copy operation. If null, default configuration is used.
A CancellationToken that can be used to cancel the asynchronous operation.
member.returns: A task that represents the asynchronous operation. The task result is true if the bulk copy succeeds.
member.exception: Thrown if the bulk copy operation fails due to an error during the import process.

member.name: M:Gurung.EfBulkOperations.PostgreSql.Handlers.PostgreSqlHandlers.CreateTempTableAsync(Npgsql.NpgsqlConnection,Npgsql.NpgsqlTransaction,Gurung.EfBulkOperations.TableDetails,System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Asynchronously creates a temporary table in PostgreSQL based on the schema of an existing table, without copying any data.
member.remarks: The temporary table is created with the same schema as the specified source table but contains no data. The operation must be performed within an active transaction and connection.
member.param: The open NpgsqlConnection to the PostgreSQL database where the temporary table will be created. Must not be null.
The NpgsqlTransaction within which the temporary table creation will be executed. Must not be null.
The TableDetails object containing information about the source table and the name for the temporary table. Must not be null.
A CancellationToken that can be used to cancel the asynchronous operation. Optional.
member.returns: A task that represents the asynchronous operation. The task result is true if the temporary table is created successfully.
member.exception: Thrown if the temporary table cannot be created in PostgreSQL.

member.name: M:Gurung.EfBulkOperations.PostgreSql.Handlers.PostgreSqlHandlers.IsDefaultValue(System.Object)
member.summary: Determines whether the specified object is equal to the default value for its type.
member.remarks: For reference types, this method returns true if the value is null. For value types, it returns true if the value is equal to the result of Activator.CreateInstance for the type.
member.param: The object to compare against the default value of its type. Can be null.
member.returns: true if the specified object is null or equal to the default value of its type; otherwise, false.

member.name: M:Gurung.EfBulkOperations.PostgreSql.Handlers.PostgreSqlHandlers.SqlCommandAsync(Npgsql.NpgsqlConnection,Npgsql.NpgsqlTransaction,System.String,System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Asynchronously executes a SQL command using the specified PostgreSQL connection and transaction.
member.param: The open NpgsqlConnection to use for executing the command. Must not be null.
The NpgsqlTransaction within which the command is executed. Must be associated with the provided connection.
The SQL command text to execute. Cannot be null or empty.
A CancellationToken that can be used to cancel the asynchronous operation. The default value is None.
member.returns: A task that represents the asynchronous operation. The task result is true if the command executes successfully.
member.exception: Thrown if the SQL command fails to execute.

member.name: M:Gurung.EfBulkOperations.PostgreSql.Handlers.PostgreSqlHandlers.ExecuteMergeAsync(Npgsql.NpgsqlConnection,Npgsql.NpgsqlTransaction,Gurung.EfBulkOperations.TableDetails,System.Boolean,Gurung.EfBulkOperations.BulkConfig,System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Executes a merge (upsert) operation on a PostgreSQL table using the specified connection, transaction, and bulk configuration.
member.remarks: This method performs an upsert by first updating existing records with matching primary keys and then inserting new records. The operation is executed within the provided transaction context. The method is intended for use with bulk data operations and relies on the configuration and table metadata supplied.
member.param: The open NpgsqlConnection to the PostgreSQL database where the merge operation will be performed.
The NpgsqlTransaction within which the merge operation will be executed. The operation is committed or rolled back with this transaction.
The TableDetails object containing metadata about the target table, including column mappings and primary key information.
true if the target table includes an identity (auto-increment) column; otherwise, false.
The BulkConfig object specifying options and settings for the bulk merge operation.
A CancellationToken that can be used to cancel the asynchronous operation.
member.returns: A task that represents the asynchronous operation. The task result is true if the merge operation completes successfully.
member.exception: Thrown if the merge (upsert) operation fails to execute in PostgreSQL.

member.name: M:Gurung.EfBulkOperations.PostgreSql.Handlers.PostgreSqlHandlers.BulkInsertOrUpdateAsync``1(Npgsql.NpgsqlConnection,Npgsql.NpgsqlTransaction,System.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable{``0},Gurung.EfBulkOperations.TableDetails,Gurung.EfBulkOperations.BulkConfig,System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Performs a bulk insert or update operation for a collection of entities in a PostgreSQL database using a temporary table and merge strategy.
member.remarks: This method uses a temporary table and a merge operation to efficiently insert new records or update existing ones based on primary key values. The operation is executed within the provided transaction and is atomic with respect to that transaction. The method does not commit or roll back the transaction; transaction management is the caller's responsibility.
member.typeparam: The type of the entities to insert or update. Must correspond to the target table schema.
member.param: The open NpgsqlConnection to the PostgreSQL database where the operation will be performed.
The NpgsqlTransaction within which the bulk operation will execute. The transaction must be valid and open.
The collection of entities to insert or update in the target table. Cannot be null.
The TableDetails object containing metadata about the target table, such as schema and column mappings.
The BulkConfig object specifying options for the bulk operation, such as batch size or column mappings. If null, default configuration is used.
A CancellationToken that can be used to cancel the asynchronous operation.
member.returns: A task that represents the asynchronous operation. The task result is true if the bulk insert or update succeeds; otherwise, false.
member.exception: Thrown if the bulk insert or update operation fails due to an error in the process.

member.name: M:Gurung.EfBulkOperations.PostgreSql.Handlers.PostgreSqlHandlers.BulkCopyToTempTableWithPKAsync``1(Npgsql.NpgsqlConnection,Npgsql.NpgsqlTransaction,System.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable{``0},Gurung.EfBulkOperations.TableDetails,Gurung.EfBulkOperations.BulkConfig,System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Asynchronously copies a collection of entities, including primary key values, to a temporary table in PostgreSQL using binary import.
member.remarks: This method is intended for scenarios where primary key values must be preserved in the temporary table, such as for insert-or-update operations. The method uses PostgreSQL's binary import for efficient data transfer. The caller is responsible for ensuring that the connection is open and that the temporary table schema matches the entity properties.
member.typeparam: The type of the entities to be copied to the temporary table.
member.param: The open NpgsqlConnection to the PostgreSQL database where the temporary table resides.
The NpgsqlTransaction to associate with the bulk copy operation. Can be null if no transaction is required.
The collection of entities to copy to the temporary table. Each entity's properties are mapped to table columns.
The TableDetails object containing metadata about the target temporary table, including column mappings and property information.
The BulkConfig object that specifies options for the bulk copy operation. If null, default configuration is used.
A CancellationToken that can be used to cancel the asynchronous operation.
member.returns: A task that represents the asynchronous operation. The task result is true if the bulk copy succeeds.
member.exception: Thrown if the bulk copy operation fails due to an error during the import process.

member.name: T:Gurung.EfBulkOperations.PostgreSql.QueryBuilder.PostgreQueryBuilder
member.summary: PostgreSQL-specific query builder for bulk operations. Generates optimized queries using COPY, INSERT ON CONFLICT, and temp table strategies.

member.name: M:Gurung.EfBulkOperations.PostgreSql.QueryBuilder.PostgreQueryBuilder.CopyCommand(System.String,System.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable{System.String})
member.summary: Generates a PostgreSQL COPY command for bulk importing data into a specified table with the given columns.
member.remarks: The generated command uses the STDIN source and binary format. This is typically used with PostgreSQL bulk import operations.
member.param: The name of the target table into which data will be imported. Cannot be null or empty.
A collection of column names to include in the COPY command. Each column name will be quoted as an identifier. Cannot be null or empty.
member.returns: A string containing the formatted PostgreSQL COPY command for the specified table and columns.

member.name: M:Gurung.EfBulkOperations.PostgreSql.QueryBuilder.PostgreQueryBuilder.GenerateUpdateQuery(System.String,System.String,System.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable{System.String},System.Collections.Generic.List{System.String})
member.summary: Generates a SQL UPDATE statement that updates rows in a target table using values from a temporary table, matching on specified primary key columns.
member.remarks: The generated SQL statement uses an INNER JOIN on the specified primary key columns and only updates rows where all primary key values in the temporary table are not null. The method assumes that column and table names are properly quoted to prevent SQL injection or syntax errors.
member.param: The name of the target table to be updated. Cannot be null or empty.
The name of the temporary table containing updated values. Cannot be null or empty.
A collection of all column names involved in the update operation, including both primary key and non-primary key columns. Cannot be null or empty.
A list of column names that make up the primary key and are used to match rows between the target and temporary tables. Cannot be null or empty.
member.returns: A string containing the generated SQL UPDATE statement that sets non-primary key columns in the target table to values from the temporary table where primary key columns match and are not null.

member.name: M:Gurung.EfBulkOperations.PostgreSql.QueryBuilder.PostgreQueryBuilder.GenerateSplitMergeQueries(System.String,System.String,System.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable{System.String},System.Collections.Generic.List{System.String},System.Boolean)
member.summary: Generates SQL queries for merging data from a temporary table into a target table using split logic for upserts and inserts.
member.remarks: The generated queries support both upsert (update or insert on conflict) for rows with existing primary keys and insert for new rows where primary keys are null or default. This method is intended for use with PostgreSQL or similar SQL dialects that support ON CONFLICT clauses. The presence of an identity column affects which columns are included in the insert query.
member.param: The name of the target table into which data will be merged.
The name of the temporary table containing the source data to merge.
A collection of all column names involved in the merge operation. The order determines the column mapping in the generated queries.
A list of column names that make up the primary key for the target table. Used to determine conflict resolution and row uniqueness.
true if the target table contains an identity column that should be excluded from certain insert operations; otherwise, false.
member.returns: A MergeQueryPair containing the generated upsert and insert SQL queries for merging data from the temporary table into the target table.

member.name: M:Gurung.EfBulkOperations.PostgreSql.QueryBuilder.PostgreQueryBuilder.GetPkNotDefaultConditions(System.Collections.Generic.List{System.String})
member.summary: Builds a SQL condition that checks whether each specified primary key column is not null and does not have its default value.
member.remarks: This method generates conditions suitable for use in PostgreSQL queries, handling common data types such as integers, UUIDs, and character types. The resulting condition can be used to filter out rows where primary key columns are either null or set to their default values, which is useful for upsert or merge operations.
member.param: A list of primary key column names for which to generate the non-default value conditions.
member.returns: A SQL WHERE clause fragment that evaluates to true only if all specified primary key columns are not null and do not contain their default values.

member.name: M:Gurung.EfBulkOperations.PostgreSql.QueryBuilder.PostgreQueryBuilder.GetPkNullOrDefaultConditions(System.Collections.Generic.List{System.String})
member.summary: Helper to build conditions for PKs that ARE null or default values.

member.name: M:Gurung.EfBulkOperations.PostgreSql.QueryBuilder.PostgreQueryBuilder.DropTempTableQuery(System.String)
member.summary: Generates a SQL statement to drop a temporary table if it exists.
member.param: The name of the temporary table to be dropped. Must be a valid SQL table identifier.
member.returns: A SQL command string that drops the specified temporary table if it exists.

member.name: M:Gurung.EfBulkOperations.PostgreSql.QueryBuilder.PostgreQueryBuilder.CreateTempTableQuery(System.String,System.String,System.Boolean)
member.summary: Generates a SQL query string to create a temporary table based on the structure of an existing source table, without copying any data.
member.remarks: The generated query uses the 'WITH NO DATA' clause to ensure that only the table structure is copied, not the data. The TEMP keyword is included only if useTempKeyword is set to true. This method does not validate the existence or validity of the provided table names.
member.param: The name of the existing table whose structure will be used to create the temporary table. Cannot be null or empty.
The name to assign to the new temporary table. Cannot be null or empty.
true to include the TEMP keyword in the CREATE TABLE statement, creating a temporary table; otherwise, false to omit the keyword.
member.returns: A SQL query string that creates a temporary table with the specified name and structure, but without any data.

member.name: T:Gurung.EfBulkOperations.PostgreSql.QueryBuilder.MergeQueryPair
member.summary: Represents a pair of SQL queries used for merging data, including queries for upserting existing records and inserting new records.
member.remarks: Use this class to encapsulate the SQL statements required for data merge operations where both upsert and insert-new logic are needed. This is commonly used in scenarios where records may either need to be updated if they exist or inserted if they are new.

member.name: P:Gurung.EfBulkOperations.PostgreSql.QueryBuilder.MergeQueryPair.UpsertQuery
member.summary: Query to UPSERT records that have existing PKs (update if exists, insert if not)

member.name: P:Gurung.EfBulkOperations.PostgreSql.QueryBuilder.MergeQueryPair.InsertNewQuery
member.summary: Query to INSERT new records that have NULL or default PKs

member.name: T:Gurung.EfBulkOperations.PostgreDataHandler.PostgreSql.PostgreSqlDataHandler
member.summary: Provides methods for performing bulk insert, update, and upsert operations on collections of entities in a PostgreSQL database using Entity Framework Core.
member.remarks: This class is designed to efficiently handle large-scale data operations by leveraging PostgreSQL-specific features and strategies. It supports bulk insert, update, and insert-or-update (upsert) scenarios, and is intended for use with Entity Framework Core DbContext instances targeting PostgreSQL. Thread safety is not guaranteed; each instance should be used within a single operation context.

member.name: M:Gurung.EfBulkOperations.PostgreDataHandler.PostgreSql.PostgreSqlDataHandler.BulkInsertAsync``1(Microsoft.EntityFrameworkCore.DbContext,System.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable{``0},Gurung.EfBulkOperations.BulkConfig,System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: This method performs a bulk insert operation for a collection of entities into a PostgreSQL database using the provided DbContext.

member.name: M:Gurung.EfBulkOperations.PostgreDataHandler.PostgreSql.PostgreSqlDataHandler.BulkUpDateAsync``1(Microsoft.EntityFrameworkCore.DbContext,System.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable{``0},Gurung.EfBulkOperations.BulkConfig,System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: This method performs a bulk update operation for a collection of entities in a PostgreSQL database using the provided DbContext.

member.name: M:Gurung.EfBulkOperations.PostgreDataHandler.PostgreSql.PostgreSqlDataHandler.BulkInsertOrUpDateAsync``1(Microsoft.EntityFrameworkCore.DbContext,System.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable{``0},Gurung.EfBulkOperations.BulkConfig,System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: This method performs a bulk insert or update operation for a collection of entities in a PostgreSQL database using the provided DbContext. Handles both new records (with null/default PKs) and existing records (with valid PKs) in a single operation. Uses a split merge strategy: UPSERT for existing records, INSERT for new records.

member.name: M:Gurung.EfBulkOperations.PostgreDataHandler.PostgreSql.PostgreSqlDataHandler.ConvertToDataTable``1(System.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable{``0},Gurung.EfBulkOperations.TableDetails)
member.summary: Converts a collection of entities to a DataTable
